subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Laniakea Supercluster
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Laniakea Supercluster.

hook.text: Our galaxy belongs to a supercluster of 100,000 galaxies called Laniakea—'immense heaven' in Hawaiian.

buildup.text: Laniakea spans 520 million light-years. All its galaxies flow toward a region called the Great Attractor.

discovery.text: Brent Tully's team mapped it in 2014 by tracking galaxy velocities. The flows revealed the supercluster's boundary.

twist.text: Superclusters aren't bound by gravity. Dark energy will eventually pull Laniakea apart over billions of years.

climax.text: The Great Attractor itself is a mass concentration hidden behind the Milky Way's dusty disk. We can only study it indirectly.

punchline.text: A hundred thousand galaxies flowing together. Our address in the cosmos.

quiz.question: What is the Great Attractor?
options: A massive gravitational concentration that galaxies in Laniakea flow toward
A supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way
A theoretical point where the Big Bang originated
